intitule: Reading Comprehension: Coping with Social Media

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions that follow.

"Social media has become a big part of our daily lives. Many young people use platforms like WhatsApp, Facebook, and Instagram to communicate with friends and family. However, there are some problems with social media. For example, some people spend too much time on their phones. Others may feel sad when they see others posting happy pictures. To cope with these problems, experts suggest limiting screen time, unfollowing accounts that make you feel bad, and spending more time with real-life friends. It is also important to think before you post and to be kind to others online."
images: An illustration showing a young person looking at a smartphone with a worried expression, with speech bubbles showing both positive (smile) and negative (sad face) social media interactions.

questions.texte: Identify two problems with social media mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: The two problems mentioned are: 1) Some people spend too much time on their phones. 2) Others may feel sad when they see others posting happy pictures.

questions.texte: Explain what experts suggest to cope with social media problems.
questions.reponse: Experts suggest limiting screen time, unfollowing accounts that make you feel bad, and spending more time with real-life friends. They also recommend thinking before you post and being kind to others online.

questions.texte: Justify why it is important to be kind to others online according to the text.
questions.reponse: It is important to be kind to others online because social media is a platform where people interact and share experiences. Being kind helps create a positive and supportive environment for everyone.

questions.texte: Analyse the advice given in the text and suggest one additional way to cope with social media problems.
questions.reponse: The advice given includes limiting screen time, unfollowing negative accounts, spending time with real-life friends, thinking before posting, and being kind. An additional way to cope could be to take regular breaks from social media, such as a 'digital detox' day each week, to reduce dependence and improve mental well-being.

intitule: Expressing Likes and Dislikes about Social Media

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences to express your likes and dislikes about social media.

questions.texte: I __________ (love / like / enjoy / hate) using social media because __________.
questions.reponse: I love using social media because it helps me stay connected with my friends and family.

questions.texte: I __________ (dislike / hate) when people __________ on social media.
questions.reponse: I dislike when people post negative comments on social media.

questions.texte: One thing I enjoy about social media is __________.
questions.reponse: One thing I enjoy about social media is watching educational videos and learning new things.

questions.texte: A problem I have with social media is that __________.
questions.reponse: A problem I have with social media is that it can be distracting and take up too much of my time.

intitule: Writing: A Short Message about Coping with Social Media

enonce.texte:
Write a short message (about 30-40 words) to a friend who is feeling sad because of social media. Give them advice on how to cope.

Your message should include:

  • A greeting
  • At least two pieces of advice
  • An encouraging closing

questions.texte: Write your short message to your friend.
questions.reponse: Hi Sarah! I saw you seemed sad on social media. Don't worry too much about what others post. Remember to take breaks and spend time doing things you enjoy. You are amazing! Take care of yourself.

intitule: Writing: A Tweet about Social Media Tips

enonce.texte: Write a short tweet (maximum 280 characters) giving advice on how to cope with social media problems.

questions.reponse: Tired of social media stress? Take a break! Limit your screen time, unfollow negative accounts, and focus on real-life connections. Remember, your mental health matters! #SocialMediaTips #DigitalWellness

enonce.texte:
Write a short dialogue (at least 8 exchanges) between two friends discussing problems with social media and how to cope with them.

Use at least three expressions of likes and dislikes (love, like, enjoy, hate, dislike).

questions.reponse:
Anna: Hey Ben! You look a bit down. What's wrong?
Ben: I've been feeling sad after seeing all the happy posts on social media.
Anna: I understand. I hate when that happens too. It makes me feel like my life isn't good enough.
Ben: Exactly! But I also enjoy seeing my friends' achievements.
Anna: That's true. But remember, social media only shows the good parts.
Ben: You're right. What do you do to cope?
Anna: I limit my screen time and unfollow accounts that make me feel bad.
Ben: That's good advice. I think I need to spend more time with real-life friends.
Anna: Yes! And take breaks from social media. You'll feel better.
Ben: Thanks, Anna! I'll try that.

intitule: Writing: A Short Chat about Social Media

enonce.texte: Write a short chat conversation (about 6-8 messages) between two friends. One friend is worried about the amount of time they spend on social media. The other friend gives advice.

questions.reponse:
Marie: I'm worried. I spend too much time on social media.
John: I understand. It can be hard to stop.
Marie: I feel like I'm wasting my time.
John: Why don't you set a timer?
Marie: That's a good idea. What else can I do?
John: Try to do other things you love, like reading or sports.
Marie: You're right. I need to be more active.
John: Yes! And remember, it's okay to take breaks. You'll feel better!
